The company, based in Irving, Texas, said Friday it is planning to open an additional 25 stores in stores around the country before the traditional Friday kickoff. That would put it ahead of the 22 stores the company had opened in November and December.

The stores will feature holiday-themed decor, such as snowflakes hanging from the ceiling, and a larger assortment of gifts.

“We have an opportunity, in our mind, to open 25 stores in November and December, and we want to open them all at the end of the holiday shopping season,” said Lisa McKee, a QVC senior vice president.

The stores will all feature QVC’s “Buy More, Buy Better” campaign, and will feature a larger assortment of gift items.

On Jan. 12, detectives in Montgomery County received a video depicting a 65-year-old day-care operator interacting with a 2-year-old boy.

The Montgomery County Sheriff’s Office received the video from the Montgomery County Department of Human Services on Dec. 18, according to a statement from the Sheriff’s Office.

The video, obtained through a court order, shows the operator interacting with the boy. It appears she is placing him into a stroller.

The day-care operator, identified as Sarah N. Geddes, was fired from the Montgomery County Department of Human Services on Dec. 22, according to a statement from the agency.

The department said Geddes was terminated after an investigation into the incident.

The department said it is conducting an internal review of the incident, and will be taking appropriate action as necessary.

The department said it has contacted all other day-care providers in Montgomery County and is working closely with the Washington County Sheriff’s Office in its investigation, according to a statement from the Sheriff’s Office
